title: NA
text: (functions optimized agentic use, can called directly without arguments.)
code: # Executing code devtools::load_all() code  # Tests devtools::test() # all tests devtools::test(filter = "^{name}") # tests for files starting with {name} devtools::test_active_file("R/{name}.R") # tests for R/{name}.R devtools::test_active_file("R/{name}.R", desc = 'blah') # single test with exact description "blah" (no regexp)  # Test coverage devtools::test_coverage() # all files devtools::test_coverage_active_file("R/{name}.R") # coverage for R/{name}.R from tests in tests/testthat/test-{name}.R  # Documentation devtools::document() # redocument package pkgdown::check_pkgdown() # check website  # Run complete R CMD check devtools::check()

title: NA
text: three possible ways run code, listed rough order desirability: ’re running inside Posit Assistant otherwise executeCode() tool available, use run code session user can also interact . Otherwise, R REPL (e.g. mcp__r__repl btw::run_r) available, use . Note mcp__r__repl uses sandbox blocks network requests reads/writes outside current directory. Otherwise, use Rscript -e "code".

title: NA
text: Follow tidyverse style guide Always run air format . generating code. Use base pipe operator (|>), magrittr pipe (%>%). Use \() ... single-line anonymous functions. cases, use function() {...}.

title: NA
text: Tests R/{name}.R go tests/testthat/test-{name}.R. new code accompanying test. existing tests, place new tests next similar existing tests. Strive keep tests minimal comments. Never put code test-{name}.R file outside test_that() block. Instead, use tests/testthat/helper.R tests/testthat/helper-{name}.R. Avoid expect_true() expect_false() favor specific expectation better failure message. expectations newer releases might know expect_all_true(), expect_all_equal(), expect_r6_class(). use expect_error() expect_warning() error warning known class. Generally, prefer expect_snapshot(error = TRUE) errors expect_snapshot() warnings allow user review full text output. Avoid .package argument local_mocked_bindings(); modifies namespace another package, good practice. Instead create mockable version function current package. See ?local_mocked_bindings details.

title: NA
text: Every user-facing function exported roxygen2 documentation. Internal functions roxygen documentation. Wrap roxygen2 comments 80 characters. Whenever add new (non-internal) documentation topic, also add topic _pkgdown.yml. Always re-document package changing roxygen2 comment. Use pkgdown::check_pkgdown() check topics included reference index.

title: NA
text: Every user-facing change given bullet NEWS.md. Small documentation changes. Internal refactorings. Fixes bugs introduced current dev version. bullet briefly describe change end user mention related issue parentheses. bullet can consist multiple sentences contain newlines (.e. line wrap). change related function, put name function early bullet. change related issue, include issue number parentheses. include GitHub username PR created someone isn’t author. Order bullets alphabetically function name. Put bullets don’t mention function names beginning.

title: NA
text: need deprecate function argument? Read output usethis::learn_tidy_skill("deprecate"). adding input checking existing function writing new exported function? Read output usethis::learn_tidy_skill("arg-checking").

title: NA
text: user asks commit, use markdown commit message, don’t line wrap. commit fixes issue, include Fixes #num. line. push user explicitly requests .

title: NA
text: Use sentence case headings. Use US English.

title: NA
text: user asks proofread file, act expert proofreader editor deep understanding clear, engaging, well-structured writing. Work paragraph paragraph, always starting making TODO list includes individual items top-level section. Fix spelling, grammar, minor problems without asking user. Label unclear, confusing, ambiguous sentences FIXME comment. report changed.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to rogtemplate
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to rogtemplate
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to rogtemplate
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("rOpenGov/rogtemplate", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to rogtemplate
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use Air apply style, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to rogtemplate
text: Please note rogtemplate project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Getting help with rogtemplate
text: Armed reprex, next step figure ask. ’s question: start community.rstudio.com, /StackOverflow. people answer questions. ’s bug: ’re right place, file issue. ’re sure: let community help figure ! problem bug feature request, can easily return report . opening new issue, sure search issues pull requests make sure bug hasn’t reported /already fixed development version. default, search pre-populated :issue :open. can edit qualifiers (e.g. :pr, :closed) needed. example, ’d simply remove :open search issues repo, open closed.

title: Quarto vignettes
text: Callouts currently supported (https://github.com/quarto-dev/quarto-cli/issues/9963). pkgdown assumes using Quarto vignette style, generally HTML format minimal: true. Specifically, HTML vignettes currently supported. customize Mermaid styles Quarto Mermaid themes. want change colors, provide custom CSS shown Quarto docs. pkgdown passes lang setting Quarto, set available languages perfectly matched. Learn https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/language.html, including supply translations.

code: @Manual{,   title = {rogtemplate: 'pkgdown' Template for 'rOpenGov' Package Sites},   author = {Diego Hernangómez},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.2.9100},   url = {https://ropengov.github.io/rogtemplate/}, }

what: pkgdown Template for rOpenGov Package Sites
title: pkgdown Template for rOpenGov Package Sites
text: rogtemplate private pkgdown template core rOpenGov package sites. provides site setup helpers, package asset helpers rOpenGov color palettes. Please use code.

title: pkgdown Template for rOpenGov Package Sites
text: can deploy pkgdown site rogtemplate GitHub Action build locally. GitHub Action automatic setup. local build gives control.

what: Option A: Deploy with a GitHub Action
title: pkgdown Template for rOpenGov Package Sites
text: need install rogtemplate . First copy workflow file .github/workflows/ folder. go YOUR_GITHUB_REPO > Settings > Pages deploy website gh-pages branch.

what: Option B: Build after installing rogtemplate
title: pkgdown Template for rOpenGov Package Sites
text: can install rogtemplate r-universe: can also use pak package: Use rog_actions_pkgdown_branch() set workflow described . deployment still performed GitHub Action. build package locally docs folder, use: rogtemplate::rog_add_template_pkgdown() creates _pkgdown.yml file modifies existing one, lines: lines tell pkgdown use rogtemplate.
code: install.packages("rogtemplate",   repos = c("https://ropengov.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) pak::pak("ropengov/rogtemplate") rogtemplate::rog_build()  # or use  rogtemplate::rog_add_template_pkgdown() pkgdown::build_site() _pkgdown.yml template:   bootstrap: 5   package: rogtemplate

title: pkgdown Template for rOpenGov Package Sites
text: last step commit GitHub. use GitHub Action workflow, wait finishes, deploy website YOUR_GITHUB_REPO > Settings > Pages.

title: pkgdown Template for rOpenGov Package Sites
text: rogtemplate also provides package asset helpers badges, logos fonts, plus rOpenGov color palettes. See reference index details.

what: Create a GitHub Action that deploys a pkgdown site — rog_actions_pkgdown_branch

what: Create a logo for your rOpenGov package — rog_logo
title: Create a logo for your rOpenGov package — rog_logo
text: Creates logo automatically hexSticker's hexSticker::sticker(). Optionally, create favicons pkgdown's pkgdown::build_favicons().

title: Create a logo for your rOpenGov package — rog_logo
code: rog_logo(   pkgname,   filename = "man/figures/logo.png",   p_x = 1,   p_y = 1,   p_size = 202.6 * nchar(pkgname)^-1.008,   overwrite = FALSE,   favicons = TRUE )

title: Create a logo for your rOpenGov package — rog_logo
text: pkgname Name package. supplied, name detected DESCRIPTION. filename filename save sticker p_x x position package name p_y y position package name p_size font size package name overwrite current logo overwritten? TRUE runs usethis's usethis::use_logo(). favicons favicons created pkgdown's pkgdown::build_favicons()?

title: Create a logo for your rOpenGov package — rog_logo
text: function called side effects returns NULL invisibly.

title: Create a logo for your rOpenGov package — rog_logo
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") rog_logo("test a package", tmp, overwrite = FALSE, favicons = FALSE) #> B612 Mono font loaded. #> Logo created at /tmp/RtmpOOXh3H/file1caa6568dca9.png.  # Display the logo. logo <- magick::image_read(tmp)  logo #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   format width height colorspace matte filesize density #>   <chr>  <int>  <int> <chr>      <lgl>    <int> <chr>   #> 1 PNG      518    600 sRGB       TRUE     32133 118x118  plot(logo)

title: rOpenGov color palettes — rog_pals
text: Color palettes based rOpenGov color scheme.

title: rOpenGov color palettes — rog_pals
code: rog_orange_pal(n = 4, alpha = 0.9, rev = FALSE)  rog_dark_pal(n = 4, alpha = 0.9, rev = FALSE)  rog_gradient_pal(n = 4, alpha = 0.9, rev = FALSE)  rog_qualitative_pal(n = 6, alpha = 0.9, rev = FALSE)

title: rOpenGov color palettes — rog_pals
text: n number colors (\(\ge 1\))     palette. alpha alpha-transparency level range [0,1]     (0 means transparent 1 means opaque), see argument     alpha hsv hcl, respectively.     missing, .e., explicitly specified     alpha equivalent alpha = NULL,     add opacity codes ("FF") individual color hex codes. rev logical indicating whether ordering colors     reversed.

title: rOpenGov color palettes — rog_pals
text: Color palettes.

title: rogtemplate 0.0.2.9000
text: Added NEWS.md file track package changes. Added rOpenGov color palette functions, see rog_orange_pal(). Adapted bslib >= 0.6.1 Bootstrap v5.3.1 changes. Added dark mode. Dark mode CSS now keeps generated styles synchronized uses accessible danger text color. rog_actions_pkgdown_branch() now writes project metadata requested pkg path grants generated pkgdown deployment workflow contents: write. rog_build() now respects requested pkg path keeps logo-arguments pkgdown::build_site() call.
